data.title: Grayscale files for BNB ETF, trading as GBNB on Nasdaq, following BNB's 36% crash from $1,363 peak. Coinbase to handle trading.
data.description: On Thursday, Grayscale submitted an application for a Binance Coin (BNB) ETF on Nasdaq under the ticker GBNB. BNB has been trading within a three-month symmetrical triangle pattern after plummeting by 36% from its peak of $1,363 in December. The ETF will consist of real BNB tokens to mirror the price movements of Binance Coin. Coinbase Global Inc. (COIN) will oversee trading and custody operations for the ETF, similar to its management of Bitcoin (BTC) and Ethereum (ETH) spot ETFs. Grayscale intends to enable large investors to create and redeem shares directly using BNB tokens instead of cash. Additionally, the fund will provide staking opportunities for investors to generate yield from their holdings.

data.title: Nordstrom to open new Nordstrom Rack in Park City, UT in fall 2026, expanding their reach to local customers.
data.description: A leading Seattle fashion retailer, Nordstrom, Inc., recently revealed its intention to inaugurate a fresh Nordstrom Rack outlet in Park City, UT during the autumn of 2026. The establishment aims to integrate with the local Park City community and cater to customers with an extensive range of high-quality brands.

data.title: Leopard Imaging will attend Embedded World and NVIDIA GTC in March 2026, showcasing high-definition vision systems.
data.description: Leopard Imaging Inc., a renowned company in high-definition embedded vision systems based in Fremont, California, revealed its upcoming involvement in two significant tech trade events in March 2026. The company will be showcasing its innovations at Embedded World in Nuremberg, Germany along with another prestigious event. This participation highlights the company's commitment to the industry, with a focus on advancing embedded vision technology.

data.title: McLean & Company provides case studies on how workplace culture impacts employee behaviors and results, helping companies address talent pressures.
data.description: Many organizations are currently experiencing challenges related to talent shortages and growing demands for enhanced employee experience. Despite this, a significant number of them find it difficult to effectively integrate their organizational culture into everyday actions. Recent case studies conducted by the renowned global HR research and advisory firm McLean & Company shed light on this issue, demonstrating the practical applications of culture within companies.

data.title: Vinted launches in New York to help people sell unworn clothes and make cash. 21% of Americans have over $500 worth of unworn clothing.
data.description: A prominent fashion resale platform from Europe, Vinted, has recently launched its services in New York. The app aims to assist individuals in converting their unused clothing into money while decluttering their homes. Statistics reveal that 21% of Americans possess unused clothing valued at over $500, which equates to a week's rent in New York City.

data.title: Cox Automotive's Dealertrack revolutionizes auto finance with new APIs and integrations for contracting and funding efficiency.
data.description: In Atlanta on January 23, 2026, Dealertrack, a brand under Cox Automotive, announced a groundbreaking initiative to revolutionize the auto finance industry. They unveiled a range of new APIs, product upgrades, and pioneering integrations aimed at boosting efficiency in contracting and funding processes for lenders and dealers. This move is set to significantly impact the industry by streamlining operations and improving overall productivity levels.

data.title: Binance's CZ predicts Bitcoin supercycle in 2026, dismisses ties to Trump. Previous trend of 4 years to end.
data.description: Changpeng "CZ" Zhao, the founder of Binance, believes that in 2026, Bitcoin will experience a supercycle, deviating from its traditional four-year trend.

data.title: Tanda and Neal Jarest discuss importance of breathable sleepwear for restorative sleep in HelloNation feature.
data.description: In Southern Pines, N.C., on January 23, 2026, Tanda and Neal Jarest from Opulence of Southern Pines delve into the significance of sleepwear on sleep quality in their spotlight with HelloNation. The piece delves into the lesser-discussed topic of how What You Wear to Sleep can greatly impact the quality of your rest.

data.title: Morgan Stanley reports Amazon's grocery business growth post-revamp, may impact Instacart, DoorDash.
data.description: According to Morgan Stanley, recent third-party data indicates a significant increase in grocery sales following a restructuring of the company. The surge in Amazon's grocery market presence is believed to be gaining momentum, raising questions about the potential impact on Instacart and DoorDash.

data.title: Intel stock falls after Q4 report, analysts say turnaround will take longer than expected.
data.description: Analysts are predicting that the turnaround of Intel will be a lengthier process than initially anticipated. Following the release of its Q4 report, Intel's stock experienced a drop on Friday. Investor expectations for the company had surpassed the current reality, as per reports.

data.title: Airlines cancel hundreds of flights as powerful winter storm approaches, affecting travel plans nationwide.
data.description: Due to the forecasted severe weather conditions, airlines are preemptively canceling hundreds of flights over the upcoming weekend. The cancellations are primarily in response to frigid temperatures and expected ice and snow impacting a significant portion of the country.

data.title: happyactually announces Dr. Andia Shahzadi as Chief Medical Officer and Founding Member.
data.description: On January 23, 2026, in Seattle, happyactually, a company specializing in wellbeing technology for life-conscious individuals, revealed the naming of Dr. Andia Shahzadi as Chief Medical Officer and Founding Member. This move underscores the company's commitment to providing evidence-based, holistic insights.

data.title: Inventor from Arkansas creates SWIFT LIFT portable bottle jack, aiming for safer and faster operation than traditional jacks.
data.description: In Pittsburgh on January 23, 2026, an inventor from Evening Shade, Ark., discussed the creation of the SWIFT LIFT, a new invention aimed at revolutionizing the operation of bottle jacks. The inventor stated that the inspiration behind the SWIFT LIFT was to enhance and modernize the efficiency of bottle jack operation. This innovative design presents a secure, rapid, and efficient substitute for traditional mechanical or pneumatic jacks. The new SWIFT LIFT is expected to significantly improve the speed and safety of lifting operations, offering a promising alternative for users seeking a more advanced solution.
